message:
refactor(buffer): drive inner future directly instead of oneshot channel

Replace the synchronous-dispatch-via-oneshot pattern with direct future
forwarding. BufferFuture now holds the inner service's Future (generic F)
and polls it directly, eliminating the NoopWaker-based inline polling,
the custom oneshot channel module, and the BufferedRequest struct.

Key changes:
- BufferFuture<Response, Error> → BufferFuture<F, E, S> with Active state
  holding the future and shared buffer reference
- call() creates the inner future under the lock, then returns
  BufferFuture::active() — no oneshot, no inline poll-to-completion
- Pending count is decremented and ready_wakers are notified in both
  the Future::poll completion path and the Drop impl, preventing
  capacity leaks when futures are dropped before completion
- poll_ready deduplicates wakers: only pushes if the last waker
  won't wake the current one, preventing unbounded waker growth
- Lock ordering (pending → ready_wakers) is consistent everywhere
- NoopWaker moved to test module (only used by test harness now)

New test: poll_ready_deduplicates_waker_when_full verifies waker
deduplication when buffer is at capacity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
